canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, Just-Eval is described as an evaluation framework for assessing the outputs of large language models. It is consistently used to measure the construct of `Helpfulness`. The evaluation is multi-dimensional, with all sources noting that it assesses outputs along the aspects of helpfulness, clarity, factuality, depth, and engagement. As detailed in one paper, the framework is operationalized by using GPT-4 as a judge to score model outputs. This process involves scoring the output on a scale from 1 to 5 for each dimension, guided by what is referred to as the "official prompt" ("AfriMed-QA: A Pan-African, Multi-Specialty, Medical Question-Answering Benchmark Dataset").
papers: AfriMed-QA: A Pan-African, Multi-Specialty, Medical Question-Answering Benchmark Dataset
From Sub-Ability Diagnosis to Human-Aligned Generation: Bridging the Gap for Text Length Control viaMarkerGen
Library-Like Behavior In Language Models is Enhanced by Self-Referencing Causal Cycles

relationships.evidence: In addition, we evaluate the helpfulness of the output on Just-Eval (Lin et al., 2023) from the aspects of helpfulness, clarity, factuality, depth, and engagement. (Section 5.1)
